description_long: This is a raised, dual-section cabin structure with two main rectangular volumes connected by a central covered walkway. The cabin features gable roofs with dark gray shingles and prominent overhangs, supported by visible wooden beams and posts. The exterior walls are light-colored, contrasted by dark-framed windows and doors, and accented with natural wood trim and railings. Both sections have spacious porches or decks, with one side featuring a wraparound porch and the other a front-facing entry with steps. The cabin is elevated on sturdy piers, suggesting suitability for uneven or flood-prone terrain. Large windows and multiple entry points provide ample natural light and accessibility.
description: A dual-section, elevated cabin with gable roofs, wood accents, and spacious porches, connected by a central walkway. The structure features large windows, light walls, and is supported by piers.
description_view_0: This view shows the cabin's two main sections, the connecting walkway, and the prominent front porch with steps, highlighting the elevation on piers and the wraparound porch on the left.
description_view_1: From this angle, the gable roofs and the depth of the porches are visible, with the front-facing windows and the symmetry of the rooflines emphasized.
description_view_2: This view displays the rear or opposite side of the cabin, showing the full length of the structure, the connecting walkway, and the extensive use of piers for elevation.
description_view_3: Here, the gable ends and the porches are prominent, with the wood railings and large windows facing forward, emphasizing the cabin's inviting entry points.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in dark asphalt shingles, supported by exposed wooden beams
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple medium-sized rectangular windows, symmetrically distributed on the front and side facades, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced individually; all have dark frames and are vertically oriented
attribute_door_placement: Two exterior doors visible: one centered on the front porch of the right section, and one on the side porch of the left section, both accessible via steps
